ddls:
CREATE TABLE "virtual_machines" (
  "vm_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each virtual machine */,
  "vm_name" TEXT /* Name of the virtual machine */,
  "data_center_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the data center where the VM is hosted */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the VM (e.g., running, stopped) */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date when the VM was created */,
  "os_type" TEXT /* Type of operating system installed on the VM */,
  "cpu_cores" INTEGER /* Number of CPU cores allocated to the VM */,
  "memory_size" INTEGER /* Memory size allocated to the VM (in MB) */,
  "disk_size" INTEGER /* Disk size allocated to the VM (in GB) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("vm_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_virtual_machines_data_center_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_center_id") REFERENCES data_centers ("data_center_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_centers" (
  "data_center_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data center */,
  "location" TEXT /* Physical location of the data center */,
  "capacity" INTEGER /* Total capacity of the data center (in VMs) */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for inquiries about the data center */,
  "address" TEXT /* Physical address of the data center */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number for contacting the data center */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_center_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "configuration_parameters" (
  "param_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each configuration parameter */,
  "param_name" TEXT /* Name of the parameter (e.g., VM.disable-console-copy) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the parameter */,
  "entity" TEXT /* Entity the parameter applies to (e.g., VM, network) */,
  "data_type" TEXT /* Data type of the parameter (e.g., boolean, integer) */,
  "default_value" TEXT /* Default value of the parameter */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("param_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "configurations" (
  "config_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each configuration entry */,
  "vm_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the virtual machine */,
  "param_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the configuration parameter */,
  "value" TEXT /* Current value of the configuration parameter */,
  "is_default" INTEGER /* Indicates if the value is default or customized */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("config_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configurations_param_id FOREIGN KEY ("param_id") REFERENCES configuration_parameters ("param_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configurations_vm_id FOREIGN KEY ("vm_id") REFERENCES virtual_machines ("vm_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "configuration_history" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each change entry */,
  "config_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the configuration entry that was changed */,
  "changed_value" TEXT /* Value that was changed */,
  "changed_date" TEXT /* Date of the change */,
  "changed_by" TEXT /* User who made the change */,
  "reason" TEXT /* Reason for the change */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configuration_history_config_id FOREIGN KEY ("config_id") REFERENCES configurations ("config_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, operator) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role (e.g., admin, user) */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions associated with the role, stored as a JSON string */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "audit_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user performing the action */,
  "action" TEXT /* Description of the action taken (e.g., created, modified) */,
  "action_date" TEXT /* Date the action was performed */,
  "resource_type" TEXT /* Type of resource affected (e.g., VM, data center) */,
  "resource_id" INTEGER /* ID of the resource affected */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audit_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "vm_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the virtual machine associated with the alert */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., configuration warning, performance issue) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the alert */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date when the alert was created */,
  "resolved_date" TEXT /* Date when the alert was resolved */,
  "resolved_by" TEXT /* User who resolved the alert */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_vm_id FOREIGN KEY ("vm_id") REFERENCES virtual_machines ("vm_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user receiving the notification */,
  "message" TEXT /* Content of the notification */,
  "sent_date" TEXT /* Date when the notification was sent */,
  "read_date" TEXT /* Date when the notification was read */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric entry */,
  "vm_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the virtual machine */,
  "cpu_usage" REAL /* CPU usage percentage */,
  "memory_usage" REAL /* Memory usage percentage */,
  "disk_io" REAL /* Disk I/O operations per second */,
  "metric_date" TEXT /* Date when the metrics were recorded */,
  "network_io" REAL /* Network I/O operations per second */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_vm_id FOREIGN KEY ("vm_id") REFERENCES virtual_machines ("vm_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "api_tokens" (
  "token_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each API token */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who created the token */,
  "token_value" TEXT /* The actual token value */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date when the token was created */,
  "expires_date" TEXT /* Date when the token expires */,
  "token_type" TEXT /* Type of token (e.g., access, refresh) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("token_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_api_tokens_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sessions" (
  "session_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each session */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user associated with the session */,
  "session_start" TEXT /* Start time of the session */,
  "session_end" TEXT /* End time of the session */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent of the user's browser or device */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("session_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sessions_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tenants" (
  "tenant_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each tenant */,
  "tenant_name" TEXT /* Name of the tenant */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date when the tenant was created */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for the tenant */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tenant_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "tenant_configurations" (
  "config_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each configuration entry */,
  "tenant_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the tenant */,
  "param_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the configuration parameter */,
  "value" TEXT /* Value of the configuration parameter */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("config_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tenant_configurations_param_id FOREIGN KEY ("param_id") REFERENCES configuration_parameters ("param_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_tenant_configurations_tenant_id FOREIGN KEY ("tenant_id") REFERENCES tenants ("tenant_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "vm_groups" (
  "group_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each group */,
  "group_name" TEXT /* Name of the group */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the group */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("group_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "vm_group_memberships" (
  "membership_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each membership entry */,
  "vm_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the virtual machine */,
  "group_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the group */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("membership_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_vm_group_memberships_group_id FOREIGN KEY ("group_id") REFERENCES vm_groups ("group_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_vm_group_memberships_vm_id FOREIGN KEY ("vm_id") REFERENCES virtual_machines ("vm_id")
)
